tactical_shape: fast and loose in buildup play
standout_players: Harry Kane 9/10 — scored twice and equalled England’s World Cup finals scoring record; played like a quarterback at times
Noni Madueke 8/10 — won the penalty and was very dangerous down the right flank
Elliot Anderson 8/10 — kept things ticking in midfield and set up Bellingham’s goal
Jude Bellingham 7/10 — gave the ball away for Croatia’s first goal but responded with a brilliant goal
Declan Rice 7/10 — delivered a pinpoint corner for Kane’s header
Marcus Rashford 7/10 — scored clinically off the bench
Bukayo Saka 7/10 — made an impact off the bench and helped create Rashford’s goal
Jordan Pickford 6/10 — made a crucial late save but looked shaky
Reece James 6/10 — dangerous going forward but poor defensively
Ezri Konsa 6/10 — improved after a difficult first half
John Stones 6/10 — rusty and nervy early, improved later
Nico O’Reilly 7/10 — got forward constantly and nearly scored
Anthony Gordon 6/10 — peripheral and unable to make an impact
Morgan Rogers 6/10 — struggled in central midfield, better higher up
Djed Spence 6/10 — should have scored soon after coming on
Marc Guéhi 6/10 — late introduction when the game was already won
fatigue_injuries: Declan Rice looks fatigued after a long season
Bukayo Saka was a surprise substitute given injury concerns
home_read.momentum: England are up and running after a thrilling opening win and a strong second-half response under pressure.

tactical_shape: open and generous defending at times
standout_players: Dominik Livakovic 9/10 — made unbelievable stops to keep Croatia in it despite an early penalty issue
Luka Modric 7/10 — still oozes class despite his age
Josip Stanisic 7/10 — lively on the right and often got the better of O’Reilly
Ivan Perisic 7/10 — clever header for the second equaliser and showed great energy
Petar Sucic 7/10 — brilliant assist for Baturina’s goal
Martin Baturina 7/10 — took his goal brilliantly and caused England problems
Petar Musa 7/10 — volleyed past Pickford and was a real handful
Andrej Kramaric 7/10 — a real handful late on
Marco Pasalic 6/10 — almost equalised after coming on
Josip Sutalo 5/10 — struggled tracking Kane
Luka Vuskovic 7/10 — talented but lost sight of Kane for England’s second goal
Josko Gvardiol 6/10 — had his hands full with Madueke
Mario Pasalic 6/10 — lost out in the midfield battle
Mateo Kovacic 6/10 — couldn’t wrestle back control
away_read.momentum: Croatia showed enough quality to trouble England and stay in the contest, but their open defending and inability to regain control left them with a narrow defeat.
